title: Kafka Connect Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on Source and Sink connectors, CDC, and SMTS.

questions.question: Which of these components moves data FROM an external database INTO a Kafka topic?

options.text: Sink Connector
Schema Registry
Debezium Source Connector
Kafka Streams App

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of log-based Change Data Capture (CDC) like Debezium over JDBC polling?

options.text: CDC is completely stateless.
CDC reads transaction logs capturing inserts, updates, and deletes immediately without querying the table.
CDC enforces rigid Avro schemas directly to the source database.
CDC translates binary SQL code into JVM bytecode.

questions.question: What happens if a worker node crashes in a Kafka Connect cluster running in Distributed Mode?

options.text: The entire cluster must be rebooted.
The connectors pause until a human restarts the node.
Tasks on the dead node are automatically rebalanced and restarted on surviving nodes.
Data in transit is deleted permanently.

questions.question: Which feature of Kafka Connect allows you to mask PII or rename a field without writing code?

options.text: Single Message Transforms (SMTs)
Converters
KStreams Topologies
Log Compaction

questions.question: What role does the Schema Registry play in Kafka Connect?

options.text: It stores credentials for database authentication.
It stores and versions the structure (like Avro/Protobuf schemas) of messages to ensure compatibility between producers and consumers.
It visualizes the DAG for Airflow orchestrators.
It assigns partitions to consumers.
